title: CSS Transitions and Animations Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties for creating smooth motion and dynamic effects on your website using CSS.

questions.question: Which of these is the MOST appropriate for creating a simple smooth change from 'state A' (normal) to 'state B' (hover)?
Which CSS rule is used to define the 'multi-step' movements for an animation (e.g., from 0% to 50% to 100%)?
For the SMOOTHEST and fastest animations, which of these properties are MOST recommended to animate because they use the computer's GPU?
What is an 'Easing Function' (like ease-in-out) used for in a transition?
options: To set the total duration of the transition.
To control how the animation accelerates and decelerates (its timing) over its duration.
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically stop the animation halfway.

questions.question: Which of these is a potential NEGATIVE effect of having TOO MANY animations and transitions on a page?
options: The site will load too fast.
It can be distracting or even nauseating for some users with vestibular disorders.
Search engines will block the site.
The browser will automatically translate the CSS into JavaScript.
